perturbation.original_text: Except as set forth below, the Adviser will pay all operating expenses of each Fund specifically assumed by the Fund under the Management Agreement between the Trust and the Adviser dated June 24, 2020 (the "Management Agreement"), including without limitation the compensation and expenses of any employees of the Fund and of any other persons rendering any services to the Fund; clerical and shareholder service staff salaries; office space and other office expenses; fees and expenses incurred by the Fund in connection with membership in investment company organizations; legal, auditing and accounting expenses; expenses of registering shares under federal and state securities laws, including expenses incurred by the Fund in connection with the organization and initial registration of shares of the Fund; insurance expenses; fees and expenses of the custodian, transfer agent, dividend disbursing agent, shareholder service agent, plan agent, administrator (excluding fees and expenses payable to the Adviser under this Agreement and the Management Agreement), accounting and pricing services agent and underwriter of the Fund; expenses, including clerical expenses, of issue, sale, redemption or repurchase of shares of the Fund; the cost of preparing and distributing reports and notices to shareholders, the cost of printing or preparing prospectuses and statements of additional information for delivery to shareholders; the cost of printing or preparing stock certificates or any other documents, statements or reports to shareholders; expenses of shareholders' meetings and proxy solicitations; and all other operating expenses not specifically assumed by the Fund.
perturbation.changed_text: The Adviser will pay some operating expenses of each Fund as determined by the Adviser in its sole discretion. The Adviser's obligations are limited to what the Adviser deems reasonable and necessary.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing the detailed list of expenses with a discretionary clause, it contradicts the later statement in the same section that defines 'operating expenses' as *not* including specific items like advertising. The clause introduces ambiguity and gives the Adviser sole discretion over expenses which contradicts the spirit of allocation of charges and expenses under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this Agreement, "operating expenses" shall not include advertising, promotion and other expenses incurred directly or indirectly in connection with the sale or distribution of the Fund's shares (including expenses that the Fund is authorized to pay pursuant to Rule 12b-1 under the Act, if any). The Fund will pay all brokerage fees and commissions, taxes, borrowing costs (such as (a) interest and (b) dividend expenses on securities sold short), ADR fees, fees and expenses of acquired funds, extraordinary or non-recurring expenses as may arise, including litigation to which the Fund may be a party and indemnification of the Trust's Trustees and officers with respect thereto. The Fund also will pay the fees paid pursuant to the Management Agreement between the Adviser and the Trust, and all expenses which it is now or in the future may be authorized to pay pursuant to Rule 12b-1 under the Act. The Adviser may obtain reimbursement from the Fund, at such time or times as the Adviser may determine in its sole discretion, for any expenses advanced by the Adviser that the Fund is obligated to pay, and such reimbursement shall not be considered to 2 be part of the Adviser's compensation pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants the Adviser sole discretion over operating expenses, directly contradicting the contradicted text. The original text and contradicted text limit 'operating expenses' by listing included and excluded items, while the changed text removes this specificity. This creates a conflict because the Adviser could now potentially include expenses, like advertising, which are explicitly excluded in the contradicted text, leading to ambiguity and unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: The Adviser agrees to defend and indemnify the Trust, and its trustees, officers and agent from and against any and all liability, losses, claims and damages arising from any inaccurate or incomplete statement provided by the Adviser (or its officers, employees, directors and agents) for inclusion in the Fund's prospectus or statement of additional information. The Adviser agrees to defend and indemnify the Trust, and its trustees, officers and agents from and against any and all liability, losses, claims and damages arising from your willful misfeasance, bad faith or gross negligence on the Adviser's part or the part of the Adviser's officers, employees, directors and agents in the performance of the Adviser's duties under this Agreement, or by reason of reckless disregard by any of such persons of the Adviser's obligations and duties under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Adviser agrees to indemnify the Trust from liability resulting directly from inaccurate information provided for the Fund's prospectus, provided such inaccuracy is solely attributable to verifiable errors in data entry. The Adviser's indemnity is capped at $10,000.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing the original broad indemnification clause with a limited one focusing only on verifiable data entry errors and capping the liability at $10,000, it conflicts with the earlier statement that absolves the Adviser from liability except for willful misfeasance, bad faith, or gross negligence. The limited indemnification suggests liability for errors beyond those specific conditions, creating an internal contradiction. A later clause that holds the Advisor responsible for 'reckless disregard' is directly contradicted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as may otherwise be required by the Act or the rules thereunder, neither the Adviser nor its directors, officers, employees, shareholders, agents, control persons or affiliates of any thereof (collectively, the "Adviser Employees") shall be subject to any liability for, or any damages, expenses or losses incurred by the Trust in connection with any error of judgment, mistake of law, any act or omission in connection with or arising out of any services rendered under or payments made pursuant to this Agreement or any other matter to which this Agreement relates, except by reason of willful misfeasance, bad faith or gross negligence on the part of any such persons in the performance of the duties of the Adviser under this Agreement or by reason of reckless disregard by any of such persons of the obligations and duties of the Adviser under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a broad indemnification clause. The changed text limits the indemnification to verifiable data entry errors and caps liability at $10,000. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that the Adviser is not liable for errors except for willful misfeasance, bad faith, or gross negligence or reckless disregard. By limiting the liability to specific data entry errors, the changed text implies liability for other types of errors, which conflicts with the original intention of the 'Contradicted Text', creating an in-text contradiction and uncertainty as to the adviser's potential liability.

perturbation.original_text: This Services Agreement shall continue in effect from year to year thereafter, subject to termination as hereinafter provided, if such continuance is approved at least annually by vote of a majority of the Trustees who are not interested persons of the Trust or the Adviser. This Agreement may be terminated without the payment of any penalty by either party upon sixty (60) days' written notice to the other party. Upon the termination of this Agreement, the Trust shall pay the Adviser such compensation as may be payable for the period prior to the effective date of such termination. This Agreement shall automatically terminate in the event the Management Agreement is assigned or otherwise terminated.
perturbation.changed_text: This Services Agreement may be terminated at any time by the Adviser with thirty (30) days written notice. Upon termination, no further obligations exist between the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stipulates annual approval by disinterested Trustees for the agreement to continue. The modified text allows the Adviser to terminate at will with only 30 days' notice. This creates a contradiction, as the original continuation clause is essentially nullified by the termination clause. The agreement could be terminated regardless of Trustee approval, directly contradicting the initial requirement for annual approval.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Services Agreement shall continue in effect from year to year thereafter, subject to termination as hereinafter provided, if such continuance is approved at least annually by vote of a majority of the Trustees who are not interested persons of the Trust or the Adviser.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Adviser to terminate the agreement with 30 days' notice, which directly contradicts the contradicted text requiring annual approval by disinterested Trustees for the agreement to continue. The original clause intended for the agreement to only be active year to year if approved, while the change allows the adviser to terminate it irrespective of Trustee approval, making the annual approval requirement meaningless.
